     The Numbers Business Report

Empire of Light (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $1,438,551Details
International Box Office $10,218,027
Worldwide Box Office $11,656,578
Further financial details...

Synopsis

At a coastal town in Thatcher’s Britain, aging Hilary Small dutifully if absently tends to the management of the locale’s beloved—albeit decaying—art deco movie theater. Her growing romantic involvement with the new hire, Stephen, a black college student, however, looks destined to run aground between the community’s racial animus and her own emotional fragility.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$163,405 (11.4% of total gross)
Legs:6.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:12.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:110 opening theaters/436 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $1,326,388

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 9th, 2022 (Limited) by Searchlight Pictures
International Releases: January 9th, 2023 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
February 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Philippines)
February 23rd, 2023 (Canceled) (Brazil)
February 23rd, 2023 (Wide), released as Empire of Light (エンパイア・オブ・ライト) (Japan)
February 23rd, 2023 (Wide) (Singapore)
Video Release: February 7th, 2023 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, language and brief violence.
(Rating bulletin 2741 (cert #53918), 7/20/2022)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Plot point: Interracial Romance, Mental Illness, Romance
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Time period setting: 1980s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

At a coastal town in Thatcher’s Britain, aging Hilary Small dutifully if absently tends to the management of the locale’s beloved—albeit decaying—art deco movie theater. Her growing romantic involvement with the new hire, Stephen, a black college student, however, looks destined to run aground between the community’s racial animus and her own emotional fragility.
